articles.title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Seems vulnerable amid suspected JPY intervention, weaker USD
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair attracts heavy intraday selling on Wednesday and dives to the 155.00 psychological mark, or the lowest level since February 24, though it lacks follow-through.

articles.title: EUR/GBP: Cross seen creeping higher – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s FX Strategy team argues that current market pricing for up to three Bank of England hikes over the next year is excessive given a loosening UK labour market and lacklustre growth.

articles.title: US, Iran reportedly getting close to a one-page memo to end the war
articles.expand: The report says that Washington is of the belief that an agreement is within reach as they are moving close to a one-page memorandum of understanding to end the war. Just take note though that the agreement here will be separate to nuclear talks, as it will also attach a framework for more detailed negotiations on the matter moving forward. As thin...

articles.title: Eurozone PMI Falls Into Contraction as Energy Shock Hits Services Hard
articles.expand: Eurozone private sector activity slipped back into contraction in April, with the Services PMI finalized at 47.6, down sharply from 50.2 in March and marking a 62-month low. Composite PMI fell from 50.7 to 48.8, its weakest reading in 17 months and the first contractionary print in nearly a year and a half. The data […] The post Eurozone PMI ...

articles.title: Eurozone economy wrestles with stagflation amid Middle East conflict, PMI data shows
articles.expand: Services PMI 47.6 vs 47.4 prelim Prior 50.2 Composite PMI 48.8 vs 48.6 prelim Prior 50.7 Trouble, trouble. The services PMI was the lowest in 62 months while the composite PMI was the lowest in 17 months as the euro area economy is hit hard from the uncertainty caused by the Middle East conflict. The decrease in business activity at the composite l...

articles.title: AUD: RBA hikes as oil shock bites – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank's Senior Market Strategist Benjamin Picton reports the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) has delivered a third consecutive 25 bp hike, taking the cash rate to 4.35% and matching the previous cycle high.

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Trades below 1.3600, nine-day EMA as bearish bias prevails
articles.expand: USD/CAD extends its losses for the second successive day, trading around 1.3590 during the early European hours on Wednesday. However, the technical analysis of the daily chart indicates the pair is positioned within the descending channel pattern, signaling an ongoing bearish bias.

articles.title: GBP/USD approaches 1.3600 amid hopes of a swift end to Iran war
articles.expand: The Pound (GBP) accelerates its recovery against the US Dollar (USD) on Wednesday, reaching session highs at 1.3595 so far, after bouncing from weekly lows near 1.3500 on Tuesday.

articles.title: Spain services sector falls into contraction territory as Middle East uncertainty weighs
articles.expand: Services PMI 47.9 vs 52.0 expected Prior 53.3 Ouch. That's a rough hit as Spain's services sector sees a marked drop in both new business and confidence. The negative impact ties to the Middle East conflict, as costs continue to rise sharply on the month and weighing on demand conditions. HCOB notes that "sales volumes were down due to a stagnant b...

articles.title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Hits eight-week highs above 0.5950 as risk appetite returns
articles.expand: The New Zealand Dollar (NZD) is rallying sharply against the US Dollar (USD) on Wednesday, as comments from US officials suggesting the war with Iran might be nearing its end have boosted investors’ appetite for risk.

articles.title: EUR/NOK: Norges Bank stance supports gradual downside – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Antje Praefcke expects Norges Bank to keep rates at 4.0% but maintain a distinctly hawkish tone compared with the Riksbank. With Norwegian inflation above target, the bank projects one or two hikes by year-end and may act in June if Iran-related risks persist.

articles.title: Brent: Strait of Hormuz risk reshapes supply outlook – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Michael Wan notes that Brent Oil has fallen below US$110 per barrel as President Trump pauses a US-led effort to help ships exit the Strait of Hormuz while talks with Iran continue.

articles.title: What are the main events for today?
articles.expand: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we get the final Services PMIs for the major Eurozone economies and the UK. The final data is rarely market-moving, so I wouldn't expect much from the releases. The data won't change anything for the respective central banks as the ECB prepares to raise rates in June unless the Strait of Hormuz is officiall...

articles.title: EUR/USD: Range holds as Iran risk dominates – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Antje Praefcke argues that geopolitical developments around the Iran conflict remain the primary driver for EUR/USD, outweighing upcoming US data such as ADP and Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) .

articles.title: Markets Flip Back to Risk-On as Trump Pauses ‘Project Freedom’, Yen Exploits Dollar Weakness
articles.expand: The market narrative has flipped again—and this time the reversal is dramatic. Just days after investors were positioning for a dangerous escalation in the Strait of Hormuz, traders are suddenly rushing back into risk assets as Washington signals that diplomacy may still be alive. The turning point came when US President Donald Trump abruptly pause...

articles.title: PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8562 (vs. estimate at 6.8160)
articles.expand: PBOC CNY reference rate setting for the trading session ahead. The 6.8562 is the strongest since March 24, 2023. The PBOC allows the yuan to fluctuate within a +/- 2% range, around this rate. PBOC injects 26bn yuan through 7 day reverse repos at unchanged rate of 1.4% This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
